query: Best way to semantically index a messy/decompiled codebase when variable names are not semantically useful
assumptions: The target corpus is messy/decompiled Roblox/Luau-like code where local variable names may be placeholders or missing.
The goal is retrieval quality for an MCP semantic-search tool, not perfect source reconstruction.
The preferred implementation should be local-first and incremental where possible.

provider_config.scholarly: arXiv, ACM/USENIX landing pages, project documentation
